title: Saving the Soil
content_html:
Saving the soil

More than a third of the Earth’s top layer is at risk. Is there hope for our planet’s most precious resource?

Section A

More than a third of the world's soil is endangered, according to a recent UN report. If we don't slow the decline, all farmable soil could be gone in 60 years. Since soil grows 95% of our food, and sustains human life in other more surprising ways, that is a huge problem.

Section B

Peter Groffman, from the Cary Institute of Ecosystem Studies in New York, points out that soil scientists have been warning about the degradation of the world's soil for decades. A single gram of healthy soil might contain 100 million bacteria, as well as other microorganisms such as viruses and fungi, living amid decomposing plants and various minerals. That means soils do not just grow our food, but are the source of nearly all our existing antibiotics, and could be our best hope in the fight against antibiotic-resistant bacteria. Soil is also an ally against climate change: as microorganisms within soil digest dead animals and plants, they lock in their carbon content, holding three times the amount of carbon as does the entire atmosphere. Soils also store water, preventing flood damage: in the UK, damage to buildings, roads and bridges from floods caused by soil degradation costs £233 million every year.

Section C

If the soil loses its ability to perform these functions, the human race could be in big trouble. The danger is not that the soil will disappear completely, but that the microorganisms that give it its special properties will be lost. And once this has happened, it may take the soil thousands of years to recover. Agriculture is by far the biggest problem. In the wild, when plants grow they remove nutrients from the soil, but then when the plants die and decay these nutrients are returned directly to the soil. Humans tend not to return unused parts of harvested crops directly to the soil to enrich it, meaning that the soil gradually becomes less fertile. In the past we developed strategies to get around the problem, such as regularly varying the types of crops grown, or leaving fields uncultivated for a season.

Section D

But these practices became inconvenient as populations grew and agriculture had to be run on more commercial lines. A solution came in the early 20th century with the Haber-Bosch process for manufacturing ammonium nitrate. Farmers have been putting this synthetic fertiliser on their fields ever since. But over the past few decades, it has become clear this wasn't such a bright idea. Chemical fertilisers can release polluting nitrous oxide into the atmosphere and excess is often washed away with the rain, releasing nitrogen into rivers. More recently, we have found that indiscriminate use of fertilisers hurts the soil itself, turning it acidic and salty, and degrading the soil they are supposed to nourish.

Section E

One of the people looking for a solution to this problem is Pius Floris, who started out running a tree-care business in the Netherlands, and now advises some of the world's top soil scientists. He came to realise that the best way to ensure his trees flourished was to take care of the soil, and has developed a cocktail of beneficial bacteria, fungi and humus to do this. Researchers at the University of Valladolid in Spain recently used this cocktail on soils destroyed by years of fertiliser overuse. When they applied Floris's mix to the desert-like test plots, a good crop of plants emerged that were not just healthy at the surface, but had roots strong enough to pierce dirt as hard as rock. The few plants that grew in the control plots, fed with traditional fertilisers, were small and weak.

Section F

However, measures like this are not enough to solve the global soil degradation problem. To assess our options on a global scale we first need an accurate picture of what types of soil are out there, and the problems they face. That's not easy. For one thing, there is no agreed international system for classifying soil. In an attempt to unify the different approaches, the UN has created the Global Soil Map project. Researchers from nine countries are working together to create a map linked to a database that can be fed measurements from field surveys, drone surveys, satellite imagery, lab analyses and so on to provide real-time data on the state of the soil. Within the next four years, they aim to have mapped soils worldwide to a depth of 100 metres.

Section G

But this is only a first step. We need ways of presenting the problem that bring it home to governments and the wider public, says Pamela Chasek at the International Institute for Sustainable Development, in Winnipeg, Canada. 'Most scientists don't speak language that policy-makers can understand, and vice versa.' Chasek and her colleagues have proposed a goal of 'zero net land degradation'. Like the idea of carbon neutrality, it is an easily understood target that can help shape expectations and encourage action. For soils on the brink, that may be too late. Several researchers are agitating for the immediate creation of protected zones for endangered soils. Whatever we do, if we want our soils to survive, we need to take action now.

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 14–17

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.
questions.blockTitle: Why soil degradation could be a disaster for humans
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
plant remains

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“living amid decomposing plants and various minerals”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Plant remains = decomposing plants.

⇒ Đáp án: minerals

_blockConfig.noteTitle: Why soil degradation could be a disaster for humans
_blockConfig.noteContent:
Healthy soil contains a large variety of bacteria and other microorganisms, as well as plant remains and [14] It provides us with food and also with antibiotics, and its function in storing [15] has a significant effect on the climate. In addition, it prevents damage to property and infrastructure because it holds [16].
If these microorganisms are lost, soil may lose its special properties. The main factor contributing to soil degradation is the [17] carried out by humans

questions.questionHtml:
Why soil degradation could be a disaster for humans

Healthy soil contains a large variety of bacteria and other microorganisms, as well as plant remains and 14_______ It provides us with food and also with antibiotics, and its function in storing 15_______ has a significant effect on the climate. In addition, it prevents damage to property and infrastructure because it holds 16_______.

If these microorganisms are lost, soil may lose its special properties. The main factor contributing to soil degradation is the 17_______ carried out by humans
questions.question_text: Healthy soil contains a large variety of bacteria and other microorganisms, as well as plant remains and __________.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
storing / climate

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“holding three times the amount of carbon...”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Đất lưu trữ carbon → ảnh hưởng khí hậu.

⇒ Đáp án: carbon
questions.question_text: It provides us with food and also with antibiotics, and its function in storing ___________ has a significant effect on the climate.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
prevent damage

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“Soils also store water, preventing flood damage”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Giữ nước → giảm thiệt hại lũ lụt.

⇒ Đáp án: water
questions.question_text: In addition, it prevents damage to property and infrastructure because it holds __________.

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
main factor

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“Agriculture is by far the biggest problem.”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Nông nghiệp là nguyên nhân lớn nhất.

⇒ Đáp án: agriculture
questions.question_text: If these microorganisms are lost, soil may lose its special properties. The main factor contributing to soil degradation is the ___________ carried out by humans.

options: A=may improve the number and quality of plants growing there.
B=may contain data from up to nine countries.
C=may not be put back into the soil.
D=may help governments to be more aware of soil-related issues.
E=may cause damage to different aspects of the environment.
F=may be better for use at a global level.
questions.groupHtml:
Questions 18–21

Complete each sentence with the correct ending, A–F, below.

Write the correct letter in boxes 18–21 on your answer sheet.
questions.explanation:
ước 1: Keyword
unused harvested crops

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“Humans tend not to return...”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Không đưa chất dinh dưỡng trở lại đất.

⇒ Đáp án: C

_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.
sentenceStems: Nutrients contained in the unused parts of harvested crops
Synthetic fertilisers produced with the Haber-Bosch process
Addition of a mixture developed by Pius Floris to the soil
The idea of zero net soil degradation
sentenceEndings: may improve the number and quality of plants growing there.
may contain data from up to nine countries.
may not be put back into the soil.
may help governments to be more aware of soil-related issues.
may cause damage to different aspects of the environment.
may be better for use at a global level.

questions.questionHtml:
Nutrients contained in the unused parts of harvested crops 18_______

Synthetic fertilisers produced with the Haber-Bosch process 19_______

Addition of a mixture developed by Pius Floris to the soil 20_______

The idea of zero net soil degradation 21_______

List of options

A. may improve the number and quality of plants growing there.

B. may contain data from up to nine countries.

C. may not be put back into the soil.

D. may help governments to be more aware of soil-related issues.

E. may cause damage to different aspects of the environment.

F. may be better for use at a global level.
questions.question_text: Nutrients contained in the unused parts of harvested crops ___

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
synthetic fertilisers

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“release polluting nitrous oxide...”
“turning soil acidic and salty”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Gây hại nhiều mặt môi trường.

⇒ Đáp án: E
questions.question_text: Synthetic fertilisers produced with Haber-Bosch process ___

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
Pius Floris mix

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“a good crop of plants emerged”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Giúp cây phát triển khỏe hơn.

⇒ Đáp án: A
questions.question_text: Addition of a mixture developed by Pius Floris to the soil ___

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
zero net degradation

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“help shape expectations and encourage action”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Giúp chính phủ chú ý vấn đề đất.

⇒ Đáp án: D
questions.question_text: The idea of zero net soil degradation ___

questions.groupHtml:
Questions 22–26

Reading Passage has sections A–G. Which section contains the following information?

Write the correct letter, A–G, in boxes 22–26 on your answer sheet.

NB You may use any letter more than once.
questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
motivation

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“best way to ensure his trees flourished...”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Động lực nghiên cứu cải tạo đất.

⇒ Đáp án: E

_blockConfig.customNote: NB You may use any letter more than once.
_blockConfig.noteContent:
**A type of parrot:**
- diet consists of fern fronds, various parts of a tree and [1]
- nests are created in [2] where eggs are laid.
**Arrival of settlers**
- the [3] of the kākāpō were used to make clothes.

questions.question_text: a reference to one person's motivation for a soil-improvement project

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
soil stayed healthy

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“nutrients are returned directly...”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Đất khỏe nhờ chất dinh dưỡng quay lại.

⇒ Đáp án: C
questions.question_text: an explanation of how soil stayed healthy before the development of farming

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
collecting information

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“field surveys, drone surveys, satellite imagery...”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Các cách thu thập dữ liệu.

⇒ Đáp án: F
questions.question_text: examples of different ways of collecting information on soil degradation

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
keeping soil safe

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“creation of protected zones”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Đề xuất lập khu bảo vệ đất.

⇒ Đáp án: G
questions.question_text: a suggestion for a way of keeping some types of soil safe in the near future

questions.explanation:
Bước 1: Keyword
difficult / overview

Bước 2: Tìm đoạn
“there is no agreed international system for classifying soil”

Bước 3: Phân tích
Không có hệ thống phân loại thống nhất.

⇒ Đáp án: F
questions.question_text: a reason why it is difficult to provide an overview of soil degradation
